articles.title: What REALLY Happened When Strickland Trained With Chimaev?
articles.description: Sean Strickland and Khamzat Chimaev trained together in Las Vegas… but the story of what happened in the gym depends on who you ask. Chimaev says he got the better of Strickland.Strickland says that’s not how it went down. So what actually happened? Normally there’s a code in MMA: what happens in the gym stays in the gym. But once both fighters start talking about it publicly, the question becomes obvious… Where are the witnesses? Extreme Couture is a packed room. Fighters, coaches, and training partners were there. Yet nobody has stepped forward to confirm either version of the story. Chael Sonnen breaks down: * why gym stories are notoriously unreliable * how “the mask” works in fighting * why training rounds can be deceptive * and why the Strickland vs Chimaev narrative may be missing key details With their potential fight looming, the mystery of those training sessions only adds more intrigue. 0:00 Strickland vs Chimaev Training Story 0:35 The Code of the Training Room 1:05 Why No One From the Gym Is Talking 1:43 Chimaev’s Media Mystery 2:36 Chimaev Training Footage Surfaces 3:32 Why Strickland Got the Fight 3:48 Dricus Du Plessis Weighs In 4:22 Is Strickland the Better Striker? 5:10 The “Mask” Fighters Wear 6:45 Why Training Rounds Can Be Misleading 7:12 Who Really Saw the Sparring? articles.title: Did Jon Jones Just Make the Biggest Mistake of His Career?
articles.description: Did Jon Jones just walk away from a massive payday? Reports say the UFC offered Jones $15 million for a fight against Alex Pereira — a matchup many believed favored the longtime champion. Instead, Jones reportedly turned it down while dealing with hip arthritis and has now challenged the UFC to release him if they believe he’s finished. Meanwhile, interim heavyweight champion Tom Aspinall continues waiting for a title unification fight. After years of inactivity and ongoing contract drama, the UFC heavyweight division may be ready to move on. Is Jon Jones really done in the UFC… or is this all part of a bigger negotiation? 👇 Let us know in the comments. #UFC #JonJones #MMA #UFCNews #MMAWeekly

articles.title: Did Jon Jones Just Make the Worst Decision of His Career?
articles.description: Jon Jones had a massive opportunity in front of him. A fight with Alex Pereira. A championship on the line. A spot on the historic White House card. And a payday reportedly worth $15 million. But Jon Jones said no. So the real question now is simple: Is Jon Jones in a better position today than he would have been if he accepted the deal? Chael breaks down: • Why Pereira was actually a favorable matchup for Jones • The negotiation mistake fighters sometimes make• How saying “no” can cause the entire deal to disappear• Why the interim heavyweight title changes everything• And how Jones could end up completely iced out of the heavyweight title picture Plus — the scenario where Pereira beats Gane, fights Aspinall… and Jon Jones may never get another shot. 0:00 Jon Jones Turns Down UFC Offer 1:20 Jon Jones vs Alex Pereira Matchup 2:30 White House Card Opportunity 3:10 Jon Jones Social Media Reaction 4:40 $15 Million UFC Payday 6:10 Interim Title & Tom Aspinall Situation 7:40 Is Jon Jones Better Off? articles.title: Francis Ngannou: “Keep the Legacy, I’ll Take the Money”
articles.description: Francis Ngannou made an interesting statement during a recent MVP press conference: "Keep the legacy… I’ll take the money." For Chael Sonnen, it was one of the most refreshing comments he’s heard in combat sports. Fighters spend years talking about their legacy, but Chael argues that legacy is something no fighter can actually control. It’s simply what people say about you long after your career is over. Ngannou’s point wasn’t that he literally doesn’t care — it’s that he understands legacy isn’t something you can manage. And when you look at Francis’ career, the idea that he avoids difficult fights doesn’t hold up. Ngannou has already fought some of the toughest names in combat sports: * Cain Velasquez * Stipe Miocic (twice) * Tyson Fury * Anthony Joshua He even agreed to fights that never happened — including a potential matchup with Rico Verhoeven. Chael explains why Ngannou’s attitude toward legacy may actually be the most honest approach a fighter can take. Because legacy isn’t an opponent. And it’s not something you can fight. articles.title: It’s Time to Bring Back Tsarukyan & Mokaev to the Octagon
articles.description: Arman Tsarukyan vs Muhammad Mokaev wasn’t just a grappling match — it was a statement. Two of the best young fighters in the sport found a way to keep their names relevant without fighting in the UFC, which is incredibly difficult to do. Muhammad Mokaev was 14-0 and ranked #3 in the world when the UFC released him. The promotion said he was “difficult to deal with,” something Mokaev says caught him completely off guard. Instead of disappearing, Mokaev has done the opposite. He’s taken wrestling matches in Russia, fights in other promotions, and now stepped in on short notice to grapple Arman Tsarukyan — giving up nearly 30 pounds in the process. Tsarukyan deserves credit too. Taking a match where the public believes you're supposed to win is never easy. If you win, you get no credit. If you lose, you hear about it forever. The result was a fast, chaotic grappling match before Tsarukyan eventually locked up the choke. But the real story isn’t who won. The story is that Mokaev is still competing everywhere he can — and Chael believes that deserves recognition. Sometimes the hardest thing in combat sports isn’t winning fights. It’s staying relevant when you’re not fighting in the UFC. And right now, Mokaev is doing everything he can to prove he belongs back. articles.description: Daniel Cormier is joined by former UFC Bantamweight champion Suga Sean O’Malley, where he talks about his upbringing in Helena, Montana, and how that type of life shaped him into the fighter he is today. Sean grew up in a city with only 30,000 people, and said he played all types of sports growing up from football, basketball, baseball and soccer. Sean talks about meeting Tim Welch when he was 17 years old, and saving up money to go train at his gym in Arizona. Tim has become Sean’s longtime coach and mentor. articles.title: The Wildest Weight Class at 2026 NCAA Wrestling Tournament
articles.description: The NCAA wrestling brackets are out, and one weight class immediately stands out: 133 pounds. This bracket is loaded with talent, controversy, and one of the most fascinating stories of the tournament — Jax Forrest. Forrest transferred directly from high school to Oklahoma State mid-season, something rarely seen in college wrestling. Despite the unusual path, he enters the tournament as the #1 seed and the #1 ranked wrestler by USA Wrestling. But the road to the title is anything but easy. Last year’s NCAA finalists Drake Ayala and Lucas Byrd are sitting at the 6 and 7 seeds, while top contenders like Marcus Blaze, Davino, and Aaron Seidel are all lurking in the bracket. Chael breaks down why this weight class could produce some of the most unpredictable matches of the entire NCAA tournament, and why the freshman class may be about to take over. articles.title: Islam Makhachev Fires Back at Ilia Topuria
articles.description: The beef between Ilia Topuria and Islam Makhachev just escalated… and now the managers are involved. What started as trash talk quickly turned into a heated exchange on social media involving Ilia Topuria, Islam Makhachev, Ali Abdelaziz, and Malki Kawa. But hidden inside the jokes and insults may actually be a major clue about why the fight between Ilia and Islam never happened. For months, fans believed the fight fell apart because Islam was injured or because the matchup didn’t make sense while Islam was moving up to welterweight. But now Islam is suggesting something different. According to his response online, Ilia Topuria agreed to fight… but only if the price was right. Chael Sonnen breaks down what Islam’s tweet might really mean, why negotiations may have fallen apart, and how the rivalry between Topuria and Makhachev could still lead to one of the biggest fights in MMA. Because from Chael’s perspective, this feud isn’t ending anytime soon. articles.title: The Night Francis Shocked the World #ufc
articles.description: I’m telling you flat out — that was one of the most remarkable sporting moments I’ve ever witnessed. Francis Ngannou stepped into a boxing ring with the lineal heavyweight king, Tyson Fury, and he didn’t just survive… he won. At least, he should have. One licensed judge scored it for Francis. A whole lot of fans scored it for Francis. Even Joe Rogan said he thought Francis won. And when that knockdown happened in Round 2? I jumped off the couch. That wasn’t luck. That wasn’t a gimmick. That was power, timing, and a fighter who refused to play the role of opponent. Let’s be clear about something: Francis wasn’t a novelty act crossing over. He was a former undisputed UFC heavyweight champion proving he belonged on the biggest stage in combat sports. So what’s the real question? Do you want to be “world champion”… Or do you want the biggest fights, the biggest moments, and the biggest paydays? Because Francis walked into enemy territory, shocked the world, and left with global respect. He changed how MMA fighters are viewed in boxing — overnight. That’s bigger than a belt. That’s legacy. And deep down, we all know what we saw. Watch it back. Tell me he didn’t win. #FrancisNgannou #TysonFury #Boxing #MMA #UFC #CombatSports #Heavyweight #FightNight #BoxingNews

articles.title: If Pereira Wins the Interim Belt… Does It Count?
articles.description: If Pereira Wins the Interim Belt… Does It Count? Is He Really Champ Champ Champ? If Alex Pereira wins at the UFC’s massive White House event, could he actually become champ-champ-champ? Chael Sonnen breaks down one of the most controversial topics in MMA: interim championship belts. Most fighters dismiss interim titles entirely. Some champions have even thrown the belt on the ground after winning it. But Chael argues something very different. History shows that interim belts often become undisputed belts. In fact, that exact scenario recently happened in the UFC heavyweight division when Tom Aspinall was elevated from interim champion to undisputed champion. So what happens if Pereira wins the interim heavyweight championship while already holding titles in other divisions? Does that count as three belts at once? Many fighters — including Conor McGregor and Henry Cejudo — don’t see interim belts the same way Chael does. But the UFC continues to create them for one reason: Because sometimes the interim champion becomes the real champion. And if that happens again, Chael says history might prove his argument right. #AlexPereira #UFC #ChaelSonnen #MMA #ChampChamp Pereira’s champ-champ-champ possibility – 00:00Why interim belts matter – 00:28Why Pereira can’t abandon the 205 belt – 01:00The debate around interim titles – 01:20Why many fighters don’t count interim belts – 01:45How Tom Aspinall became undisputed champion – 02:10Why interim champions sometimes get promoted – 02:40The scenario where Pereira becomes undisputed – 03:20Why Chael believes interim belts still count – 04:00The risk around the current heavyweight situation – 04:30Why history often repeats itself in MMA – 05:10The Ilia Topuria interim belt example – 05:50Why Chael believes Gaethje is a multi-time champion – 06:20Why the interim belt at the White House matters – 06:50 Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Fact: Stars Are No Longer Needed in UFC...
articles.description: Don’t sleep on @ultrapouches. New customers get 50% Off with code CHAEL at takeultra.com! #UltraPouches #adbad Jake Paul recently claimed that the UFC is dying. But Chael Sonnen says the reality is much more complicated. With the UFC securing an $8 billion media rights deal, the company is entering a completely new era — one where the traditional star-driven pay-per-view model may no longer apply. For decades, combat sports revolved around a few massive draws like Conor McGregor, Jon Jones, Tito Ortiz, and Randy Couture. These fighters carried entire events and were responsible for generating the majority of revenue. But the sport may be shifting into something entirely different. Instead of needing superstar fighters to sell pay-per-views, the UFC may now be operating on a numbered event model where the promotion itself — not the fighters — is the main attraction. Chael explains why this shift could completely change how fighters build careers, how promotions structure events, and why even the biggest stars in the sport may no longer be required to headline every card. Meanwhile, Jake Paul’s MVP promotion is experimenting with a different strategy altogether — potentially building fights around crossover athletes like Francis Ngannou and even future boxing matchups with Jake Paul himself. So is the UFC dying? Or is the sport simply evolving into a new business model nobody fully understands yet? #UFC #JakePaul #ChaelSonnen #MMA Timestamps Jake Paul says the UFC is dying – 00:00Why the UFC’s $8B deal changes everything – 00:10The new “numbered event” era – 00:24Why nobody truly understands the new model – 00:40How the pay-per-view era worked – 01:00Why stars like McGregor and Jon Jones were essential – 01:30The early UFC model with Tito and Randy – 01:50Why most fighters were never needed – 02:10How the WWE business model influenced the UFC – 03:00Why the sport is shifting away from stars – 04:00How fighters manipulated the star system – 04:20Why O’Malley, Adesanya and Pereira became draws – 04:40Why Jake Paul’s MVP depends on UFC-built stars – 05:00The new financial reality of the UFC – 05:20Why the company now knows its revenue years ahead – 05:40Can MVP survive without Jake Paul fighting? – 06:10How Francis Ngannou may set up a Jake Paul fight – 06:40Why crossovers between boxing and MMA could grow – 07:10The brutal truth of the new fight business – 08:00 Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: “The White House Card Sucks”? You’re Wrong #ufc
articles.description: “The UFC White House card sucks.” I keep hearing it. And I genuinely don’t understand it. What exactly were you expecting — magic? They gave you the biggest stage imaginable and stacked it with the biggest stars in the sport. That’s not opinion. That’s fact. You’ve got Ilia Topuria — one of the fastest-rising names in MMA — taking on the kind of challenge fans beg for. You’ve got Alex Pereira moving into a brand-new weight class with a title opportunity. That alone is massive. New division. Real stakes. Global spotlight. And somehow… he’s not even the main event. That tells you everything. Add in Sean O'Malley — one of the sport’s most recognizable stars — and now you’re talking about three of the biggest draws in MMA on one card. Star power. Storylines. Title implications. New weight class drama. What else could you realistically ask for? This isn’t a regional show. This isn’t filler. This is marquee talent on a historic stage. Some fans just like complaining more than they like fights. If you care about elite talent, championship stakes, and crossover stars, this card delivers across the board. Watch it. Then tell me it “sucks.” More fight breakdowns, bold takes, and real MMA talk coming your way. #UFC #MMA #IliaTopuria #AlexPereira #SeanOMalley #UFCCard #FightNight #CombatSports #MMANews

articles.title: Ronda Rousey Was SAVAGE at the Press Conference
articles.description: Ronda Rousey showed up to the press conference and did exactly what she was supposed to do. And Chael Sonnen loved every second of it. During the heated media event promoting the massive Ronda Rousey vs Gina Carano fight, Rousey didn’t hold back. She fired shots at the UFC, called out Valentina Shevchenko, and even took direct jabs at her opponent sitting right next to her. It was raw. It was uncomfortable. It was classic Ronda. But according to Chael, that’s exactly how it should be. If you’re part of a promotion, you pick a side and back your team. Rousey came representing Jake Paul’s MVP promotion, and she made it crystal clear she wasn’t there to praise the UFC. Still, Chael pushes back on one major claim Ronda made: that the UFC is the worst place for fighters to compete. Chael explains why the reality of the fight business may actually prove the opposite. From fighter pay to contracts, bonuses, and the structure of the sport, the UFC may still be the most stable and reliable place in all of combat sports. And while Ronda may have been cutting a promo, she definitely succeeded in doing one thing: Getting everyone talking again. #RondaRousey #UFC #ChaelSonnen #MMA #GinaCarano #DanaWhite 0:00 Ronda Rousey’s fiery press conference 0:15 Why Chael loved Ronda’s attitude 0:45 The promo against Valentina Shevchenko 1:20 Ronda’s comments about Gina Carano 2:45 Is the UFC really the worst place to fight? 3:10 What other MMA promotions exist today 4:00 Why MVP is playing a different game 5:10 Chael’s problem with the criticism of the UFC 6:00 Dana White’s impact on fighter contracts 7:15 The truth about fighter pay and bonuses 8:10 Why Ronda’s promo worked perfectly Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench
